welcomeCard.subtitle: Intelligent Green Space Management
welcomeCard.description: Learn how we optimize the irrigation of urban green spaces using smart sensors and data-driven analysis — for healthier trees, flower beds, and parks with efficient resource usage.

contactHero.intro: We look forward to hearing from you

email.title: Write to us
email.description: Do you have questions about the project or would you like to learn more? We will get back to you shortly.

github.title: Open Source
github.description: Explore our code, contribute to development, or use Green Ecolution for your municipality.

homepageHero.label: Smart Irrigation
homepageHero.description: Using sensor-based monitoring of green spaces, data is transmitted and analyzed through the LoRaWAN network, enabling actionable irrigation recommendations.

irrigationStatus.label: Measuring irrigation status
irrigationStatus.description: Soil moisture is measured at different depths depending on vegetation type to gather data across multiple soil layers.

dataTransmission.label: Data transmission
dataTransmission.description: Sensor data is transmitted via the public LoRaWAN network to a backend for further processing. LoRaWAN requires little energy and provides long range coverage, resulting in large area coverage and low maintenance effort.

recommendations.label: Recommendations for action
recommendations.description: The transmitted sensor data is analyzed using scientific and mathematical methodologies. A dashboard visually displays whether irrigation will be necessary in the near future.

adoptTreeModal.location: is waiting for you in Flensburg
adoptTreeModal.quote: “Thank you for thinking of me! Projects like Green Ecolution finally give city trees like me the attention we deserve.”

funFacts.cooling: A large urban tree can evaporate up to 400 liters of water per day and cool its surroundings by up to 3°C.
funFacts.oxygen: A single beech tree produces enough oxygen for 10 people per year.
funFacts.dust: Urban trees filter a significant portion of fine dust from the air in their immediate surroundings.
funFacts.roots: The roots of a street tree often extend over one and a half times the size of its crown area.
funFacts.co2: A 100-year-old tree has stored around 5 tons of CO₂ during its lifetime.
funFacts.woodWideWeb: Trees communicate through underground fungal networks — the so-called “Wood Wide Web”.
funFacts.lifetime: Street trees live an average of only 60 years — in rural areas they live three times longer.
funFacts.noise: Dense tree populations can reduce noise levels by up to 10 decibels.
funFacts.water: In hot summers, an urban tree may require up to 200 liters of additional water per week.

errorFallback.title: Something went wrong
errorFallback.description: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again or return to the homepage.
